fact: March 10 is exactly 69 days into the year - that's almost 19% of the whole year already gone!
In the Northern Hemisphere, maple trees typically start producing sap around this time for syrup making - they're basically tree vampires!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around this time, like nature's perfect compass.
Earthworms often start emerging from deep underground right around now in northern climates - it's like a slimy underground parade!
March 10 falls in the middle of Pisces season, when the zodiac fish are supposedly swimming around being all dreamy.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are typically turning orange and red around this time - it's autumn backwards!
Daylight is growing by about 3 minutes every day in northern places - that's like gaining a whole TV commercial of sunlight daily!
Bears in cold regions often start waking up from hibernation around this time, probably really cranky with terrible morning breath.
This date sits exactly 11 days before the spring equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal like a cosmic balance scale.
In many northern places, the ground is still frozen on top but starting to thaw underneath, creating a weird ice sandwich effect.
Migrating birds are typically flying overhead in huge flocks around this time, following invisible highways in the sky that only they can see.
The moon phase around March 10 changes every year, but it's always doing something dramatic - either growing fatter or shrinking skinnier each night.
